title: endotropis auriculata
intro: Endotropis auriculata, also known as the "ear-tipped bean," is a species of plant in the legume family. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat various infections, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. In herbal preparations, Endotropis auriculata is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and stems are also used in traditional remedies.
